{"query":"Novak Djokovic French Open 2026 Joao Fonseca match result score","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2162,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7318309/2026/05/29/novak-djokovic-french-open-draw-joao-fonseca/","title":"João Fonseca stuns Novak Djokovic at French Open from two sets down - The Athletic","content":"Jun 3, 2026 — João Fonseca knocked him out of the French Open Friday night coming from two sets down to win 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 in four hours and 53 ...
PARIS — Novak Djokovic’s hopes of a record-breaking 25th Grand Slam are over — for now.
Rising Brazilian teenager João Fonseca knocked him out of the French Open Friday night in a five-set thriller, coming from two sets down to win 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 in four hours and 53 minutes.
At 39 years old, Djokovic may never get a better opportunity."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/sport/tennis/articles/c1521xnnxewo","title":"French Open 2026 results: Novak Djokovic beaten by Joao Fonseca in third-round thriller - BBC Sport","content":"May 29, 2026 — Fonseca battled back from 3-1 down in the fifth set to win 4-6 4-6 6-3 7-5 7-5 after a gruelling four hours and 53 minutes.
Novak Djokovic's wait for a record 25th Grand Slam singles title goes on after teenage star Joao Fonseca produced a stunning fightback to win a five-set epic in the French Open third round.
But after winning the first two sets, Djokovic was gradually overpowered by 19-year-old Fonseca, who hit back to force a decider.
In front of a raucous crowd, Fonseca battled back from 3-1 down in the fifth set to win 4-6 4-6 6-3 7-5 7-5 after a gruelling four hours and 53 minutes.
He is also joined by second seed and 2024 runner-up Alexander Zverev, who beat Quentin Halys in four sets, and 19-year-old Rafael Jodar - who has emerged as a genuine title contender after winning 18 of his 21 clay-court matches in 2026.
Djokovic came out fighting after taking a lengthy break before the decider but, although he struck the first blow, breaking Fonseca in game four, it was the teenager - playing a fifth set for just the third time in his career - who rose to the occasion as the match reached its crescendo."},{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/en/news/french-open-2026-novak-djokovic-beaten-in-five-set-nail-biter-by-joao-fonseca","title":"French Open 2026: João Fonseca living the dream after beating his "idol" Novak Djokovic","content":"In a match for the ages between the tennis legend and the emerging star, the Brazilian youngster came from two sets down to advance to the fourth round.
Novak Djokovic gave his all at the French Open 2026 on Friday (29 May) but eventually fell in a gruelling five-set, third-round marathon against João Fonseca.
With the men’s bracket of the tournament blown open after Jannik Sinner’s shock defeat, another huge star exited the tournament as the Paris 2024 gold medallist lost 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 to the 19-year-old Brazilian, who went on the attack after going two sets down.
Fonseca said afterwards: "He’s such an idol, and it was a pleasure just stepping on to the court against him.
Back and forth between the pair, the Serbian came within two points of victory before Fonseca responded to claw a game from deuce once more.
Norway's Casper Ruud saved two match points in the fourth set and advanced to the fourth round after defeating Tommy Paul in five sets, while second-seed Alexander Zverev of Germany defeated France's Quentin Halys in four sets."},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/sports/tennis/novak-djokovic-joao-fonseca-french-open-results-rcna347601","title":"Djokovic’s 25th Slam bid ends in a grueling French Open loss to Brazilian teenager","content":"May 29, 2026 — The 39-year-old Serb — a three-time champion at Roland Garros — lost 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 as temperatures again reached 33 degrees C (91 F).
Novak Djokovic’s latest quest for a record 25th Grand Slam was ended by Brazilian teenager Joao Fonseca in the third round of the French Open on Friday.
Djokovic looked exhausted in the fifth set but soldiered on and his exit leaves the men’s draw even more open following Jannik Sinner’s second-round exit on Thursday."},{"url":"https://www.cbssports.com/tennis/news/2026-french-open-results-novak-djokovic-upset-joao-fonseca-roland-garros/","title":"2026 French Open results: Joao Fonseca stuns Novak ...","content":"May 29, 2026 — Novak Djokovic was ousted in a five-set thriller (4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5) by 19-year-old rising Brazilian star Joao Fonseca on Court"}]}

A day after Jannik Sinner — the men’s top-ranked player — fell at the 2026 French Open, another highly ranked competitor suffered a premature defeat. Novak Djokovic was shockingly eliminated from the event Friday, losing in five sets to Joao Fonseca. After a 6-3 win in the third set, Fonseca won the fourth set 7-5. While Djokovic desperately tried to tie things up, he lost the final game 7-5, giving Fonseca the win in both the set and the match. With the win, the 19-year-old Fonseca advanced to the fourth round of the French Open for the first time in his career. For Djokovic, it’s the first time since 2009 he’s failed to advance past the third round of the French Open. After picking up wins in the Australian Open, Wimbledon and the U.S. Open by 2011, it took Djokovic until 2016 to win the French Open and secure his first career Grand Slam.","score":0.83932644,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.flashscore.com/match/tennis/djokovic-novak-AZg49Et9/fonseca-joao-tv073LUc","title":"Joao Fonseca v Novak Djokovic results, H2H stats | Tennis","content":"Winners 70 47 … 2nd serve points won 53%(25/47) Fonseca celebrates his French Open win Joao Fonseca incredible French Open victory over Novak Djokovic","score":0.7728226,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/48912435/djokovic-25th-slam-bid-ends-french-open-loss-fonseca","title":"Djokovic's 25th Slam bid ends with French Open loss to Fonseca - ESPN","content":"PARIS -- There will be a new men's champion at the French Open after Novak Djokovic followed Jannik Sinner out of Roland Garros in a five-set stunner on Friday. Brazilian teenager Joao Fonseca beat 24-time major winner Djokovic 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 in the third round to follow Thursday's huge upset, when No. 1 Sinner -- last year's runner-up lost to 56th-ranked Juan Manuel Cerundolo. Djokovic's latest quest for a record 25th Grand Slam singles title was ended and it was just the second time he lost from two sets up, the other also coming in Paris in 2010. In the final game, Djokovic had a break point for 6-6 but Fonseca served out with three consecutive aces and became the first teenager to beat Djokovic at a Grand Slam tournament. Djokovic said he was unsure if he would play at the French Open next year, although he said the same after his semifinal defeat to Sinner last year.","score":0.7265231,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.01,"request_id":"12ceff8a-1d9d-4c9e-a694-bb990c298947"}
